meaning: n.魔鬼,魔王,撒旦,邪神,能手,成癖者
examples: Hitherto he had thought it was some fiend fighting him, but darker suspicions assailed him now.
A tearful husband repeated calls for help in catching the fiend who battered his wife.
"I will soon seize you," said the fiend.
Exxon for a few years held the position as arch-fiend after the Valdez spill.
History has remembered Rasputin as an almost supernatural fiend, but he was in reality one of the century's great political fixers.
